feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Zoomers obsession with "so bad it's good" content
entry.content: A daily reminder that "so bad it's good" culture propagated by companies like Vinegar Syndrome, RLM "Best of the Worst", Sam Hyde, Doug Walker, and MST3K is terrible for the soul and one of the tactics they use to demoralize a population. Whether you participate in Option A or Option B, you are pure scum: Option A >"Don't you get it? Buying VINEGAR SYNDROME MOVIES is being part of an INSIDE JOKE!!!!" >"LOl look at the trailer and all the awkward guttural laugh moments in the movie. How incompete

entry.title: /b/ Random: >But I have to have a gun! It's in the Constitution. >Dad, the 2nd Amendment is …
entry.content: >But I have to have a gun! It's in the Constitution. >Dad, the 2nd Amendment is just a remnant from revolutionary days. It has no meaning today. >You couldn't be more wrong, Lisa. If I didn't have this gun, the king of England could just walk in here anytime he wants and start shoving you around. Do you want that? Huh?

entry.content: >Always wanted to try WOW, but don't want to give Actiblizzard money >Try out a private server that goes up to WOTLK >Surprised to see it was so easy to pick up and play. You qualify for SSIs if you have trouble getting into old WOW outside of quality of life like bagsorting >For comparison, Everquest 2 still feels jank today from a beginner's standpoint. E.g: where's the UI scale option? >Shaman surprised me by having tech with the totems >Early maps feel like just the right size with the right

entry.title: /int/ International: Fatties of /int/ You ARE going to lose weight in the summer, right? I'm going to…
entry.content: Fatties of /int/ You ARE going to lose weight in the summer, right? I'm going to do it with swimming and when summer ends I will continue maintaining with jogging. You have no fucking excuse. Swimming doesn't penalize overweight like jogging does. You can rapidly lose weight this way. It's perfect for fatties. I have July and August to become thin. LET'S FUCKING GO. Swimming 5-6 days a week unless there is thunder or some shit.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Help me
entry.content: My ex-girlfriend cheated on me with a generic local club promoter and told me my music is absolute trash and I will never amount to anything. I spent the last 3 months locked in my room working on this single track just to prove that bitch wrong. I want to make this viral so she sees it everywhere. Please bump and share this if you want to help a fellow anon ruin his ex's mood. Is it actually bad? Honest thoughts?

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Right-wing Americans dream of Europe becoming one 'white' mass with no distincti…
entry.content: Right-wing Americans dream of Europe becoming one 'white' mass with no distinctions between nation-states. They want it to become like America, which is, every european should have some blood of a German, a little bit French, a little bit Finnish, Bulgarian, Serbian, Anglo-Saxon, Italian, Portuguese, and Russian. For them the idea of nationalism seems to be unthinkable. For them, only the idea of 'racionalism' exists.

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: For all the newfags: You're only supposed to check dubs if the person first call…
entry.content: For all the newfags: You're only supposed to check dubs if the person first calls them in advance and tells you to check them. Dubs are not rare. Roughly every 10th post is going to have dubs. It only becomes somewhat noteworthy if the poster correctly calls their repeating digits in advance. Otherwise, you're just pointlessly noting the naturally occurring repeating digits that happen all the time. I see a lot of people improperly checking dubs that were not called in advance, which shows that

entry.title: /o/ Auto: New Car Tech Just Dropped
entry.content: >all new American vehicles will have full 360 cameras inside and outside the car >cameras will do facial and retinal scan of all those inside and outside the car and compile the data in a national record and cross reference it with a criminal database >steering wheels and dashboards will have fingerprint censors Basically it's a good thing because it can protect you from car thieves. If somebody tries to break into your car, their biometrics will be identified. If your car is successfully stolen

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Cream Thread
entry.content: Why is Cream so underrated these days? I know Clapton's covid comments made him a pariah amongst Rolling Stone writers (a big worshiper of Clapton/God) but even still, they removed Cream's albums from the greatest of all time list. Not that many Spotify listeners (same with Jimi Hendrix). I started playing guitar in 2009 after hearing Clapton on the Steppin Out from the Live Cream II album. I couldn't tell you then why I liked Cream so much then, but listening back now, after getting into Jazz m

entry.content: /flg/ - F-List General #8342 Fox Edition Previous Thread: >>83686018 F-list is a website for people interested in erotic roleplaying, and creating character profiles to find like-minded partners with similar interests and fetishes to roleplay with. It features a webchat (which can be used for textual roleplaying), group forums and more kinks and depravity than you can memorize. The purpose of this thread is to serve as a place where roleplayers can freely share and advertise their profiles, soli

entry.title: /b/ Random: Am I cucked or just buying porn?
entry.content: So long story. There’s a lot of back story to this as well, but I’m going to cut a lot of that as it’s a lot to articulate. When I was 19 I was a major loser following high school, lost many friends and had nothing much going for me, I took a wild swing at trying to get with my old high school crush, and to my surprise she actually started talking to me, she’s quite autistic and was struggling to make friends at college. After a few months of talking she was all over me, it’s like I became her h

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What are some good ways to promote your app?
entry.content: I created an app to help Catholics read the bible and learn the faith, but I don't know how to promote it. Much of reddit limits "promotional" posts, so I don't know how I can get users. The application that automatically helps you read the bible and study the Catholic faith. The application is free. It has the full Catholic 73 book bible in automatic reading mode (if desired). It also has a Learn section which, right now, consist of the traditional teachings in the Baltimore Catechism. There's

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: DMC5's level design is both by far the series' best AND the most representative …
entry.content: DMC5's level design is both by far the series' best AND the most representative of what people always claim to want out of it, in the sense that it's the only game in the series with levels which can be called non-linear in any sense of the term (since several levels have multiple routes through them and/or objectives which can be completed in different orders). Arguments as to why this isn't the case genuinely just stem from people not liking how they look and pretty much nothing else. Presenta

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >No longer need to scour the internet for Reddit forums and youtube tutorials, c…
entry.content: >No longer need to scour the internet for Reddit forums and youtube tutorials, can simply just ask GPT 5 a step by step tutorial for most tech related trouble shooting I hate AI, but can't deny this is extremely useful.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Why wasn’t Armie Hammer a bigger star?
entry.content: Before his cancellation he was never more than B-list. It’s strange because he’s a very good actor, he has leading man looks and he’s from a very privileged background >His father, Michael Armand Hammer, owned several businesses, including Knoedler Publishing and Armand Hammer Productions, a film/television production company. Hammer has described his background as "half Jewish". His paternal great-grandfather was oil tycoon and philanthropist Armand Hammer, whose parents were Jewish emigrants t

entry.title: /int/ International: Egyptian welfare overhaul
entry.content: The Egyptian ministry of finance recently announced a complete overhaul of the old in-kind welfare system we've had since the 70s, planning to move to a money-based system where the government automatically deposits a lump sum (50-350L.E per citizen; it's still in the talks) into special "welfare cards" with the money only spendable at government-sponsored vendors. A 70g loaf of bread will stay at 0.20L.E, while cooking oil, (30L.E/bottle) sugar (15L.E/kg) and all other goods (soap, detergent, p

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Why are there no unreliable 5 cylinder engines?
entry.content: Whether it's gas or diesel, or whether it's Volvo VW/Audi, Mercedes-Benz or even FIAT, every single 5 cylinder engine seems to be extremely reliable, especially compared to 4 and 6 cylinder engines

entry.title: /b/ Random: I'm a guy almost 40. A woman smiled at me while I was walking out of the gas sta…
entry.content: I'm a guy almost 40. A woman smiled at me while I was walking out of the gas station today and I didn't smile back. Not because I didn't want to, but because I always have this nervous feeling in public, and I've caught myself doing the "resting bitch face" thing. Anyway, I feel bad about it and I have no one to tell this to.. So I'm posting it here. Sorry lady. You were cute. It wasn't your fault. Please keep smiling at other guys. Dammit, I feel bad about this. I'm going to go mow the yard...

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >Gibbs's cover version of Lavern Baker's R&B hit reached #2 on the Billboard in …
entry.content: >Gibbs's cover version of Lavern Baker's R&B hit reached #2 on the Billboard in 1955 and aroused a considerable amount of controversy over whitewashed covers of black pop. The arrangement from the original Baker recording on Atlantic was lifted wholesale by Mercury, but despite her protests, Baker discovered that song arrangements were not covered by US copyright law--this was a settled matter since the "A Little Birdy Told Me" case a few years earlier when a US district judge in Los Angeles rul

entry.title: /g/ Technology: AI tech CEOs and governments love global control. It doesn't matter what company…
entry.content: AI tech CEOs and governments love global control. It doesn't matter what company or country they run, their goal is centralizing power. So, why don't we try to build a truly human-like AI using nothing but pure code and mathematics? The concept is simple: we just need to try and accurately represent every human aspect—cognition, logic, and architecture—strictly through math and algorithms
